architecture_summary: BoTNet replaces the final three spatial 3 × 3 convolutions in a ResNet with Multi-Head Self-Attention (MHSA) layers over low-resolution featuremaps, while keeping the rest of the backbone unchanged. The paper also introduces BoTNet-S1 for image classification by using stride 1 in the final MHSA layers and scaling the model family to larger image sizes and capacities.

training_details.compute_surface: up to 1.64x faster in “compute” time than the popular EfficientNet models on TPU-v3 hardware

novel_contributions: BoTNet, a conceptually simple yet powerful backbone architecture that incorporates self-attention for multiple computer vision tasks including image classification, object detection and instance segmentation.

Replacing the spatial convolutions with global self-attention in the final three bottleneck blocks of a ResNet and no other changes improves the baselines significantly on instance segmentation and object detection while reducing the parameters, with minimal overhead in latency.
ResNet bottleneck blocks with self-attention can be viewed as Transformer blocks, and the paper names this block Bottleneck Transformer (BoT).
BoTNet achieves 44.4% Mask AP and 49.7% Box AP on COCO Instance Segmentation using Mask R-CNN, surpassing the previous best published single model and single scale results of ResNeSt.
BoTNet achieves 84.7% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet while being up to 1.64x faster in compute time than EfficientNet models on TPU-v3 hardware.

key_claims.claim: BoTNet is a simple backbone architecture that replaces the final three spatial convolutions in a ResNet with global self-attention and improves detection and segmentation with minimal latency overhead.
BoTNet achieves 44.4% Mask AP and 49.7% Box AP on COCO Instance Segmentation using Mask R-CNN, surpassing the previous best published single model and single scale results of ResNeSt.
BoTNet-based image classification models achieve 84.7% top-1 accuracy on ImageNet and are up to 1.64x faster in compute time than EfficientNet models on TPU-v3 hardware.
Replacing the final three 3 × 3 convolutions in a ResNet with MHSA layers forms BoTNet, and the architecture reduces parameters relative to ResNet-50 in Table 1.
BoT50 improves over R50 on COCO instance segmentation across longer training schedules, with the largest gains appearing in the 3x and 6x settings.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.1. BoTNet improves over ResNet on COCO Instance Segmentation with Mask R-CNN

key_claims.claim: Relative position encodings improve performance, and combining qk^T with qr_{relative}^T gives additive gains over the baseline.
BoTNet improves backbones in the ResNet family and BoT101/BoT152 outperform their ResNet counterparts on COCO.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.4. BoTNet improves backbones in ResNet Family

key_claims.claim: BoTNet scales well with larger images, and BoT200 reaches 49.7 AP^{bb} and 44.4 AP^{mk} on COCO.
key_claims.evidence_section: 4.5. BoTNet scales well with larger images
